creator: brett@peloton.runet.edu
text:
Hi,

On Sat, 6 Nov 1999 jay.krell@cornell.edu wrote:

> >How-To-Repeat: Do a clean install of FreeBSD 3.3 from the CD, don't
> do much/any network configuration, select the tk/tksetiathome (not
> sure of exact name), package. >Fix: Not sure. The ports collection is
> a good idea, but it seems you open yourself up to a lot of this sort
> of thing, and I'm not sure you have enough control over it to fix it.

So you've done no network set up and you want to complain that the tkseti
won't run because it can't find a valid email address????  

If you don't have networking set up, how on earth do you plan to
contribute to a distributed network computing project?

In short, this is not a problem w/ the port, but that the machine isn't
set up correctly yet.

Brett
